RESULT OF AGM
Star announces the result of voting on the resolutions at its AGM held today, Thursday 18 June 2026, as set out in the AGM notice.
A poll was held on each of the resolutions and was passed by the required majority. Resolutions 1 to 10 were passed as ordinary resolutions, Resolutions 11 and 12 were passed as special resolutions. The results of the poll were as follows:

latest.title: Director/PDMR Shareholding
latest.content:
28 May 2026 09:43
RNS Number : 0606G
Star Energy Group PLC
28 May 2026
28 May 2026
Star Energy Group plc
("Star Energy" or the "Company")
Director/PDMR Shareholding
Star Energy (AIM:STAR) announces that Ross Glover, Chief Executive Officer, and Frances Ward, Chief Financial Officer, sold an aggregate of 350,016 ordinary shares of 0.002 pence each in the Company ("Ordinary Shares") to satisfy tax and national insurance liabilities arising from the exercise of options as announced on 26 May 2026.
Ross Glover has sold 289,541 Ordinary Shares and therefore, following this transaction, he is interested in 845,803 Ordinary Shares representing 0.44% of the issued share capital of the Company.
Frances Ward has sold 60,475 Ordinary Shares and therefore, following this transaction, she is interested in 320,474 Ordinary Shares representing 0.17% of the issued share capital of the Company.
The notification below, made in accordance with the requirements of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) No 596/2014 which forms part of UK law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018, provides further detail.

latest.title: Exercise of Options and Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
26 May 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 5198F
Star Energy Group PLC
26 May 2026
26 May 2026
Star Energy Group plc
("Star Energy" or the "Company")
Exercise of Options and Total Voting Rights
Star Energy (AIM:STAR)
announces that it has been notified that Ross Glover, Chief Executive Officer, and Frances Ward, Chief Financial Officer, have exercised options over an aggregate of 777,812 ordinary shares of 0.002 pence each in the Company ("Ordinary Shares") at nil cost pursuant to the 2015 Management Retention Plan and the 2016 Executive Incentive Plan (the "Option Shares"). To satisfy the option exercise, 777,812 new Ordinary Shares will be issued ("New Ordinary Shares"). The New Ordinary Shares will be admitted to trading on AIM pursuant to the Company's block listing admission facility announced on 21 May 2026.
Ross Glover has exercised 643,424 Option Shares and therefore, following this transaction, he is interested in 1,125,870 Ordinary Shares representing 0.58% of the issued share capital of the Company.
Frances Ward has exercised 134,388 Option Shares and therefore, following this transaction, she is interested in 371,475 Ordinary Shares representing 0.19% of the issued share capital of the Company.
Following the exercise, Ross Glover and Frances Ward intend to sell Ordinary Shares to cover tax and national insurance liabilities.
Total Voting Rights
The issued share capital of the Company following the above issue of New Ordinary Shares will be 192,642,955 Ordinary Shares; the number of deferred shares
remains 303,305,534.
The total number of voting rights in Star Energy will be 192,642,955 Ordinary Shares, which should be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in Star Energy under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
The notification below, made in accordance with the requirements of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) No 596/2014 which forms part of UK law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018, provides further detail.

company_30s.what_they_do: STAR.L is a small‑cap renewable‑energy developer that builds and operates geothermal plants, mainly in Croatia. The group also explores other low‑carbon technologies, but geothermal remains its flagship asset.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Geothermal is one of the few truly carbon‑neutral power sources that can operate 24/7. As Europe tightens emissions rules, the demand for steady, low‑cost geothermal electricity is growing. STAR’s projects sit on high‑grade geothermal reservoirs that could feed the national grid or export power, giving it a niche that larger utilities can’t easily replicate.
company_30s.current_state: The company has recently sold its Croatian geothermal unit and is raising up to £0.6 m through a retail offering to shore up its balance sheet. It is still in a pre‑revenue stage for its next project, relying on debt and equity to fund construction. The share price is near zero, reflecting a steep decline from its all‑time high.

rns_narrative: STAR has been busy restructuring: it just announced the sale of its Croatian geothermal asset, filed a new corporate presentation, and is launching a retail offering to raise fresh capital.
The AGM results and option‑exercise filings suggest the board is tightening the financials and attempting to reduce leverage.

Collectively, these moves paint a picture of a company that is shedding under‑performing assets, cutting costs, and trying to raise enough cash to stay afloat while positioning itself for future projects.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quietly cautious: investors are watching the capital raise, but most chatter is muted or neutral.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: None visible – the few bullish posts are generic corporate‑presentation comments that don’t signal a breakout.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Some traders fear the company’s debt load and the fact that it has sold its only operating asset. They worry the retail offer will fail and the company will run out of cash.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has shifted from a handful of neutral comments in May to a handful of cautious bullish posts in June, but overall engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no public search interest, which means a surprise catalyst could create a big gap.

near_term_catalysts: The completion of the £0.6 m retail offering – if the offer closes, the company will have a fresh cash buffer and the market may see a price bump.
The next earnings release (expected mid‑July) – a positive surprise on cash flow or a new project announcement would provide a clear bounce.
A volume spike above 1 M shares coupled with a break above the 30‑day moving average would signal renewed interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden increase in daily volume, a price break above the 10‑p support level, and any mention of a new project or partnership in press releases or on Twitter.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early stages of a crash‑recovery cycle. The APEX score indicates a tight compression, but the lack of a historic bounce means we’re still waiting for the first sign of capitulation to end.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The company is a classic deep‑crash candidate – its price is down well over 70% from its peak. If the retail offering succeeds and the next earnings beat expectations, the share could climb to the 50% of its pre‑crash level, delivering roughly 200% upside. The mean‑reversion thesis is strong because the asset base is still valuable and the market is increasingly supportive of geothermal.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the retail offer falls short or the company fails to secure new project financing, the cash burn could push the stock into a further decline. The worst case is a 90% drop from today, which would leave investors with almost no residual value.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 200% (if price reaches the 50% pre‑crash level). Potential downside: 90% (if the company runs out of cash). The asymmetry favors a contrarian position, but the risk remains high.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a confirmed break above the 10‑p support with volume > 500k shares. Size the position so that a 5‑p stop (≈50% of current price) limits loss to 25% of the trade value. Use a trailing stop at 10% below the peak to lock in gains as the price climbs.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for the retail offer to close and for the first earnings call. If the company reports positive cash flow or a new project announcement, consider a small entry to test the waters.
playbook.if_passing: No genuine terminal risk detected – the company is not delisted or bankrupt. Therefore, there is no immediate reason to abandon the position if the above catalysts materialise.

ai_insights.bottom_line: STAR.L sits at a steep crash bottom with a clear restructuring plan; a successful retail offering or a positive earnings surprise could trigger a sharp rebound, but the company’s high debt and uncertain future projects keep the downside real.

commentary.executive_summary: This report runs in HISTORICAL-ONLY mode: triangulation (RNS/social/trends) not loaded. The setup shows TOO_EARLY timing with 48/100 APEX score. Historical data shows 2 rallies averaging 19% upside. Current position: -5.8%. Your edge comes from waiting for AI Engine confirmation instead of blind entry.

bear_case: TOO_EARLY means you can get chopped before real move
commentary.timing_translation: It might be starting, but not proven yet. Wait for base + volume + catalysts.
commentary.confidence_explained: Confidence 40/100 reflects full data quality with triangulation loaded.
